info.title: QubicBob API
info.description: REST API for QubicBob - a distributed ledger indexer and query node for the Qubic network.

get.description: Returns current node status including processing tick, epoch, and connection info.
Returns the balance and transfer counts for a Qubic identity.
Returns asset ownership information for a specific identity.
Returns information about a specific epoch including initial tick.
Returns logging events within an ID range for a specific epoch.

Returns all log events emitted during the virtual end-of-epoch tick of the given epoch. The end-of-epoch tick runs after the last regular tick of the epoch and contains protocol housekeeping logs (e.g. dividend distribution, epoch transition events).

post.description: Search for logging events by SC index, log type, and topics within a tick range.
Get custom logging events by SC index, log type, epoch, and tick range with optional topic filters.
Returns all QU transfers involving a specific identity within a tick range.
Returns all transfers of a specific asset involving a specific identity within a tick range.

post.summary: Get all transfers of an asset
post.description: Returns all transfers of a specific asset within a tick range.
Execute a read-only query on a smart contract. Returns immediately if cached, otherwise returns pending status.

202.description: Query pending - retry with same nonce

400.description: Invalid request or no BM connection

post.description: Broadcast a signed transaction to the Qubic network.

400.description: Invalid transaction or no BM connection

post.description:
Single HTTP POST endpoint that accepts a JSON-RPC 2.0 request envelope. The `method` field selects the operation, and `params` carries the method-specific arguments (almost always a single-element array — either a primitive or a filter object).

Swagger UI users: pick a method from the **Examples** dropdown below to see a fully filled-in request body.

Response shapes vary per method. For full per-method documentation of result fields, error codes, constraints, and Ethereum-equivalent mappings, see [QUBIC_JSON_RPC.md](../docs/QUBIC_JSON_RPC.md).

**Methods grouped by domain:**
- **Chain info:** `qubic_chainId`, `qubic_clientVersion`, `qubic_syncing`, `qubic_status`, `qubic_getCurrentEpoch`
- **Tick:** `qubic_getTickNumber`, `qubic_getTickByNumber`, `qubic_getTickByHash`, `qubic_getTickLogRanges`
- **Transaction:** `qubic_getTransactionByHash`, `qubic_getTransactionReceipt`, `qubic_broadcastTransaction`, `qubic_sendRawTransaction` (alias of broadcast)
- **Account / Balance:** `qubic_getBalance`
- **Transfers:** `qubic_getTransfers`, `qubic_getQuTransfers`, `qubic_getAssetTransfers`, `qubic_getAllAssetTransfers`
- **Asset:** `qubic_getAssetBalance`, `qubic_getAssets`
- **Logs:** `qubic_getLogs`, `qubic_findLogIds`, `qubic_getLogsByIdRange`, `qubic_getEndEpochLogs`
- **Epoch:** `qubic_getEpochInfo`
- **Network:** `qubic_getComputors`
- **Smart Contract:** `qubic_querySmartContract`
- **Subscriptions (WebSocket only):** `qubic_subscribe`, `qubic_unsubscribe` — calling these over HTTP returns a METHOD_NOT_FOUND error directing you to `/ws/qubic`.

qubic_clientVersion.summary: qubic_clientVersion - get node software version string

qubic_syncing.summary: qubic_syncing - returns false when fully synced, otherwise sync progress object

qubic_status.summary: qubic_status - node status (optional challenge param echoes back signed)

qubic_getTickLogRanges.summary: qubic_getTickLogRanges - map tick numbers to logId ranges (params[0] = array of ticks, max 1000)

qubic_getTransactionReceipt.summary: qubic_getTransactionReceipt - receipt with logs emitted by the transaction

qubic_broadcastTransaction.summary: qubic_broadcastTransaction - submit a hex-encoded signed transaction

qubic_getBalance.summary: qubic_getBalance - QU balance for a 60-char Qubic identity

qubic_getTransfers.summary: qubic_getTransfers - generic log filter (identity, fromTick, toTick, scIndex, logType, topic1-3, epoch)

qubic_getQuTransfers.summary: qubic_getQuTransfers - QU transfer hashes for identity in tick range (max 1000 ticks)

qubic_getAssetTransfers.summary: qubic_getAssetTransfers - transfers of one asset involving one identity (max 1000 ticks)

qubic_getAllAssetTransfers.summary: qubic_getAllAssetTransfers - all transfers of an asset (no identity filter, max 1000 ticks)

qubic_getAssetBalance.summary: qubic_getAssetBalance - positional form: [identity, issuer, assetName, manageSCIndex?]

qubic_getAssets.summary: qubic_getAssets - list all assets owned by an identity

qubic_getLogs.summary: qubic_getLogs - filter logs by tick range, identity, logType, etc.

qubic_findLogIds.summary: qubic_findLogIds - efficient ID-only filter (required: scIndex, logType, fromTick, toTick)

qubic_getEndEpochLogs.summary: qubic_getEndEpochLogs - logs from end-of-epoch virtual tick

qubic_getEpochInfo.summary: qubic_getEpochInfo - epoch metadata (initial tick, end tick, log ranges)

qubic_getComputors.summary: qubic_getComputors - computor list for epoch (defaults to current)

qubic_querySmartContract.summary: qubic_querySmartContract - async SC read; resend same nonce to poll for result

qubic_subscribe.summary: qubic_subscribe - WebSocket only. Over HTTP returns METHOD_NOT_FOUND. Shown here for reference (tickStream example).

qubic_unsubscribe.summary: qubic_unsubscribe - WebSocket only. Pass the subscription id returned by qubic_subscribe.

200.description: JSON-RPC response envelope. Contains either a `result` (on success) or an `error` object (on failure). The HTTP status is always 200 for well-formed JSON-RPC requests, even when the embedded `error` is set.

get.description:
**WebSocket endpoint** for Qubic JSON-RPC 2.0 subscriptions.

Connect using a WebSocket client (e.g., `wscat -c ws://localhost:40420/ws/qubic`).

## Protocol

All messages follow the JSON-RPC 2.0 format.

### Client → Server: Subscribe

**Subscribe to new ticks:**
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "qubic_subscribe", "params": ["newTicks"]}
```

**Subscribe to logs (with optional filters and catch-up):**
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "qubic_subscribe", "params": ["logs", {
  "identity": "BZBQ...",
  "logType": [0, 1],
  "startLogId": 12345,
  "startEpoch": 152
}]}
```

**Subscribe to QU transfers (with optional catch-up):**
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "qubic_subscribe", "params": ["transfers", {
  "identity": "BZBQ...",
  "startLogId": 12345,
  "startEpoch": 152
}]}
```

**Subscribe to tick stream (with optional filters and catch-up):**
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "qubic_subscribe", "params": ["tickStream", {
  "startTick": 12345678,
  "skipEmptyTicks": true,
  "includeInputData": true,
  "txFilters": [{"from": "BZBQ...", "to": "AABB...", "minAmount": 1000, "inputType": 0}],
  "logFilters": [{"scIndex": 0, "logType": 0, "transferMinAmount": 100}]
}]}
```

### Client → Server: Unsubscribe

```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 2, "method": "qubic_unsubscribe", "params": ["qubic_sub_1"]}
```

### Server → Client: Subscribe Response

Returns the subscription ID:
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "result": "qubic_sub_1"}
```

### Server → Client: Subscription Event

```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "qubic_subscription", "params": {"subscription": "qubic_sub_1", "result": {...}}}
```

### Server → Client: Catch-Up Complete

When a subscription with catch-up (startLogId or startTick) finishes replaying historical data, a completion notification is sent:
```json
{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "qubic_subscription", "params": {"subscription": "qubic_sub_1", "result": {"catchUpComplete": true}}}
```
After this message, all subsequent events are real-time.

## Subscription Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `newTicks` | Receive tick data as new ticks are verified |
| `logs` | Receive log events matching filter criteria |
| `transfers` | Receive QU transfer events (logType=0) with optional identity filter |
| `tickStream` | Receive full tick data with filtered transactions and logs |

## Catch-Up Behavior

Subscriptions that specify `startLogId` (for logs/transfers) or `startTick` (for tickStream) will replay historical data before switching to real-time. During catch-up:
- Log/transfer events include `"isCatchUp": true`
- TickStream events include `"isCatchUp": true` in each tick object
- A `catchUpComplete` notification is sent when all historical data has been delivered
- Events that arrive during catch-up are queued and delivered in order before switching to real-time

400.description: Bad Request - Not a WebSocket upgrade request

endTick.description: End-of-epoch virtual tick (0 if epoch not yet closed)

endTickStartLogId.description: First log ID in the end-epoch tick (-1 if not available)

endTickEndLogId.description: Last log ID in the end-epoch tick (-1 if not available)

lastIndexedTick.description: Last tick that has been fully indexed

latestLogId.description: Latest processed log ID. Live counter for current epoch, endTickEndLogId for past epochs. -1 if no data.

nonce.description: Unique identifier for this query (use same nonce to poll for results)

message.example: Query enqueued; try again with the same nonce

jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC protocol version. Must be exactly "2.0".

id.description: Request identifier. Echoed back in the response. May be a string, number, or null. Omit for notifications.

method.description: JSON-RPC method name. See the operation description for the full list and link to the QUBIC_JSON_RPC.md reference for per-method response shapes.

params.description: Positional parameter array. Most methods take a single object as params[0]; some take positional primitives. See the example dropdown for per-method shapes.

id.description: Echoes the id from the request.

result.description: Method-specific result. Present on success. Shape varies per method - see QUBIC_JSON_RPC.md for full schemas.

error.description: Present on failure. Mutually exclusive with result.

code.description: JSON-RPC error code (e.g. -32600 invalid request, -32601 method not found, -32602 invalid params, -32603 internal error).

tags.name: JSON-RPC
tags.description: Ethereum-like JSON-RPC 2.0 API. Single POST endpoint that accepts a JSON-RPC request envelope; the method field selects the operation.
